name: Spiced Pear Butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine pears, wine, and lemon juice in a heavy large saucepan. Cover and simmer until pears are soft, pushing unsubmerged pears into the liquid occasionally, about 25 minutes.
2. Force through a food mill or coarse sieve to remove pear peel. Transfer to a processor and puree. Return puree to the heavy large saucepan.
3. Add remaining ingredients. Stir over low heat until sugar dissolves. Increase heat to medium and boil gently until mixture thickens and mounds slightly in a spoon, stirring often, about 50 minutes.
4. Discard fruit slices, cloves, vanilla pod, and cinnamon stick. Spoon butter into hot canning jars, filling only to 1/4 inch from the top. Immediately wipe rim with a towel dipped in hot water. Place lid on jar; seal tightly.
5. Repeat with remaining jars. Arrange jars in a large pot. Add boiling water to pot so that at least 1 inch of water covers the tops of the jars. Cover pot and bring to boil rapidly for 15 minutes.
6. Remove jars from pot. Cool to room temperature. Press center of each lid. If it stays down, the jar is sealed. (If the lid pops up, store butter in the refrigerator.) Store in a cool, dry place up to 1 year. Refrigerate after opening.
